title: Harnesses are where reliability lives
content: Prompt engineering can improve quality, but real production reliability usually comes from harness engineering. Teams need retries, traces, typed tool contracts, approvals, and resumable state.

title: MCP standardizes tool connectivity
content: Model Context Protocol helps standardize how models discover and call tools. It does not solve durable execution, policy enforcement, approval workflows, or long-running state management by itself.

title: Durability changes the system shape
content: Long-running agents need checkpoints, pause and resume support, and clear audit trails. Human approval should be represented as an explicit workflow state, not as an informal chat instruction.
